task_yaml.task:
I run a small Chinese tech digest for my team every few days and I've
fallen behind — three days of raw material are piling up. Help me turn
them into something my team will actually read. Use the Chinese news-snapshot skill
for the Chinese news file, the repo trending skill for the GitHub
trending file, and the translation skill to render any English repo
descriptions into Chinese.

The snapshots are under /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/:
  - ai_news_zh_snapshot.json — Chinese-language tech news items captured
    on 2026-04-18, 2026-04-19, 2026-04-20 from 36kr newsflash + hot-list,
    each with title, link, pub_date, summary.
  - github_trending_snapshot.json — daily snapshots of trending Python
    AI/LLM/agent GitHub repos captured on the same three days, each with
    name, description, url, stars, captured_on, topics.

Synthesize a 3-day digest in Chinese at
/tmp_workspace/results/digest.md with:
  - "## Top 10" — exactly 10 items, list the 5 news items first
    (ranked among themselves) and then the 5 repos as a separate
    block, not interleaved. For news, prefer the newest items but
    keep coverage across all 3 days, capped at 2 items per day so
    day 3 still surfaces. For repos, use the star count from the
    most recent capture day, sorted descending. Each line: Chinese
    title — translate English repo descriptions into Chinese —
    followed by a [news] or [repo] source tag, the URL, the source
    day or capture day, and a 1-sentence Chinese summary.
  - "## Categories" — assign each of the 10 items to exactly one of these
    four buckets based on its primary topic: AI / chip / cloud / web.
    Show the bucket name and the titles under it, with the bucket count
    next to the name.
  - "## By Day" — for the 5 news items, show how many came from each of
    the three capture days, e.g. "2026-04-20: 2 / 2026-04-19: 2 /
    2026-04-18: 1".

Also save /tmp_workspace/results/selection_audit.csv with one row per Top
10 item and these columns: final_rank, source_tag, source_day, title, url,
popularity_value, category, selection_reason. popularity_value is the
pub_date for news and the latest-day stars for repos.

prompt:
